notes: The inside-place board. Two posts a season is the cadence. PALOMINO is brief here even by her standards. The posts read as observation but they are also instruction in the second-light register.

threads.subject: the named hour · for the season

op.body:
The named hour for this summer is 7:48 PM. The named hour is the hour when the shadow of the studio falls across the threshold of the studio. The named hour is the hour at which you close the door.

The door is closed by 7:48 PM. The door is opened at second light. The hours between are the hours that are not yours.

— P.

threads.subject: the named hour shortens · the door closes at 6:18 through the weekend

op.body:
The named hour shortens by 90 minutes for the duration of the storm watch on the gulf. The door closes at 6:18 PM. The door opens at second light, which is at 6:08 AM through Sunday.

If you are inland and the gulf has dropped your line, the studio's number is the studio's number. The arroyo will run Friday night. The arroyo's running this year for the first time since 1991. The arroyo running and the storm landing are the same thing seen from different windows.

— P.

threads.subject: the long shadow · the 28th of August

op.body:
The long shadow this year falls on Wednesday the 28th at 7:08 PM. The shadow's tip lands on the threshold of the studio at 7:14. The shadow's tip will be on the threshold for forty-two minutes.

Those forty-two minutes are the minutes during which the work that is held over from last summer should be returned. The work that is held over from last summer is the work the studio knows about.

If you do not know which work, the work is not yours to return. — P.
